title: Sium tenue
intro: Sium tenue, also known as slender water parsnip, is a perennial aquatic plant that belongs to the carrot family (Apiaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help in treating digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Sium tenue include coumarins, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Sium tenue is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in the production of tinctures and capsules, although its use is largely anecdotal and requires further scientific research for confirmation of its efficacy.
taxonomy: Sium tenue is classified in the family Apiaceae, commonly known as the carrot or parsley family. This family is a part of the order Apiales and the clade known as the asterids. Sium tenue is a member of the genus Sium, which contains a limited number of species. The genus Sium is further classified as part of the subfamily Sineonoideae. The classification of Sium tenue is a part of the larger system of plant taxonomy.
